rag_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid component type or implementation scopes based on the scope of the referenced ports or bindings.
Generated AGREE code must not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the analyzed model.
generic_rules: AGREE assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same annex.
AGREE scalar type declarations must use int, real, or bool; avoid package-qualified or invalid type names.
When AGREE reports type mismatches, align helper declarations with the expression types (e.g., bool for comparisons).
If AGREE diagnostics reference unresolved identifiers, trace and fix the root cause or remove the invalid clause.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are used for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes for bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types that are not declared in the AADL model or its dependencies.
Do not reference subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
